entailments: national vitality, conflict, or war cost is represented through blood and cleansing
National renewal and war cost are understood through body-blood imagery.
cmt.rival_reading: The country, race, or conflict is not literally blood that can be cleansed.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule 'wwi-blood-national-renewal' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

entailments: collective resolve or service is framed through body parts, soul, or embodied strength
National service is understood through embodied agency.
cmt.rival_reading: The phrase applies bodily or inner-life language to collective political commitment.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule 'wwi-embodied-national-service' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

entailments: wartime obligation is represented as a physical burden to carry
Allied responsibility is understood through bodily carrying.
cmt.rival_reading: The obligation is political and military, not a literal carried object.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule 'wwi-burden-obligation' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.
